title: Lakshmi Stotra
intro: A divine hymn dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, the goddess of prosperity and wealth, recited for abundance and blessings.
description:
The Lakshmi Stotra is a sacred composition that honors the divine qualities of Goddess Lakshmi and seeks her blessings for prosperity, health, and well-being.

|| Stotra ||
Om Mahapadme Hridayam, Tava Padmapalasini.
Ananta Kalyana Gunaih, Paripur Navamedini.

This stotra is commonly recited during Lakshmi Puja and Diwali celebrations.
meaning: The Lakshmi Stotra celebrates the goddesses of wealth and fortune. Regular recitation is believed to bring prosperity, remove obstacles, and ensure abundance in all aspects of life.

faq.q1: When should Lakshmi Stotra be recited?
faq.a1: Especially during Diwali, Fridays, and during puja times for maximum benefit.
faq.q2: What material should one use while reciting?
faq.a2: Traditionally recited facing east with flowers and incense, but pure intention matters most.
faq.a3: By invoking the divine grace and aligning one's consciousness with abundance and generosity.
